category_reason: system/internal signal or no positively user-visible signal (expected All 5 'verify content type negotiation' scenario checks pass against the documented behaviour, observed [api-tester-verify-content-type-negotiation] TIMEOUT after 1800s)
sub_test: Aggregate failure: verify content type negotiation (5/5 scenario checks failed)
title: [verify-content-type-negotiation] Aggregate failure: verify content type negotiation (5/5 scenario checks failed) on n/a — aggregate finding across endpoints — expected All 5 'verify content type negotiation' scenario checks pass against the documented behaviour, observed [api-tester-verify-content-type-negotiation] TIMEOUT after 1800s (undocumented)
expected: All 5 'verify content type negotiation' scenario checks pass against the documented behaviour

testing_steps: 1. Start the target API at http://localhost:8899.
2. Write a script that sends GET http://localhost:8899/products with Accept: application/json, text/html and application/xml, and POST /products/add with Content-Type: application/json vs text/plain.
3. Assert the documented outcome per combination (JSON body + correct response Content-Type; unsupported types rejected with the documented 406/415).
4. Compare your script's failures with the per-scenario evidence in TestCases/verify-content-type-negotiation/cases.json — this run recorded 5 of 5 checks failing.

description: The verify-content-type-negotiation check failed in aggregate against http://localhost:8899: [api-tester-verify-content-type-negotiation] TIMEOUT after 1800s. The per-scenario evidence is in TestCases/verify-content-type-negotiation/cases.json. There is no single reproducible API call — reproduce it with the script described in the steps below (which is also why this bug has no Postman section).
steps_to_reproduce: 1. Start the target API at http://localhost:8899.
2. Write a script that sends GET http://localhost:8899/products with Accept: application/json, text/html and application/xml, and POST /products/add with Content-Type: application/json vs text/plain.
3. Assert the documented outcome per combination (JSON body + correct response Content-Type; unsupported types rejected with the documented 406/415).
4. Compare your script's failures with the per-scenario evidence in TestCases/verify-content-type-negotiation/cases.json — this run recorded 5 of 5 checks failing.
